general news.headline: CBSE admits 2 Class XII students marked on wrong answer sheets, to revise marksCBSE acknowledged discrepancies in multiple Class 12 answer sheet cases after students raised concerns online. Live: Quad becoming forum that can act on problems facing world, says US's RubioQuad Foreign Ministers Meeting Live Updates: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar hosted his counterparts from the US, Japan and Australia in New Delhi for the Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting, a key strategic gathering taking place amid growing geopolitical flux across the Indo-Pacific. Convened under India's chairmanship, the meeting will bring together US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong and Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi to review regional security challenges and expand cooperation across critical sectors. Building on deliberations held in Washington in July 2025, the four nations are expected to discuss the evolving Indo-Pacific security architecture, assess progress on ongoing Quad initiatives, and strengthen collaboration in areas such as maritime security, emerging technologies, connectivity, disaster response and economic resilience. With intensifying great-power competition and rising instability across parts of Asia and West Asia, the New Delhi meeting is being closely watched as a significant marker of the Quad's efforts to reinforce its role as a strategic platform for a "Free and Open Indo-Pacific." Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

US military hits IRGC boats, missile launcher in Iran amid peace talksAmid a ceasefire, the US military carried out 'self-defence' strikes" in southern Iran, targeting missile launch sites and IRGC boats allegedly laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM said the operation aimed to protect US forces, adding the limited strikes do not signal the end of the ceasefire.

The Samarkand Syndrome: Why Babur and family never became 'us'For the Timurids, or the Mughals of Hindustan, Samarkand remained their home, which they lost and could never get back. Bharat became the stage upon which their grandeur unfolded, while their imagination repeatedly drifted beyond the Hindu Kush toward the orchards, graves and memories of their homeland in Central Asia.

After Garib Nagar demolition, Mumbai's train-window gaze turns towards more slumsThe Garib Nagar demolition in Mumbai's Bandra suburb has triggered calls to clear more slums across the city, with viral videos shot from local train windows fuelling demands for similar drives in Wadala, Kurla, Dadar and beyond. But these settlements also house the workers Mumbai depends on.

Live: Congress to hear Siddaramaiah, Shivakumar in one-on-one meetings, say sourcesKarnataka Leadership Buzz Live Updates: Speculation over a leadership change and Cabinet reshuffle in Karnataka intensified after Chief Minister Siddaramaiah and Deputy CM DK Shivakumar confirmed their Delhi visit for meetings with the Congress high command on Tuesday 11 am. Siddaramaiah said he was invited by the party leadership but was unaware of the agenda, while Shivakumar confirmed he would travel to the national capital after initially saying he would go only if called. Several senior ministers are also expected in Delhi. The development comes amid renewed chatter over a possible leadership transition after the Congress government completed three years in office on May 20. Besides the CM face-off, the high command is also expected to discuss a Cabinet reshuffle, Rajya Sabha polls and concerns within the party over the impact of prolonged uncertainty on governance and preparations for the 2028 Assembly elections. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates on this crucial political story.

mAadhaar to be discontinued, govt announces new Aadhaar app. Details hereThe government said the mAadhaar app will soon be discontinued. UIDAI's new Aadhaar app will have upgraded security and QR-based verification features for safer digital identity access.

Live: Explosions reported near Bandar Abbas in IranUS-Israel-Iran Talks Live Updates: Senior Iranian officials Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi arrived in Doha on Monday for talks with Qatar's prime minister as efforts continued to secure a possible US-Iran agreement aimed at ending the three-month conflict and reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The negotiations are currently focused on two major issues -- restoring navigation through the strategically vital waterway and resolving disputes over Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium. However, both Washington and Tehran played down expectations of an immediate breakthrough. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio, speaking in New Delhi, said diplomacy would be given every opportunity but warned the US would pursue another way if negotiations failed. US President Donald Trump also stressed that no deal had been finalised and insisted the US naval blockade around Iran would remain in place until a formal agreement was signed. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said progress had been made on several issues but cautioned that the sides were still not close to signing any agreement. The proposed 14-point framework reportedly centres on ending hostilities and reopening the strait. Oil prices dropped sharply on Monday amid cautious optimism over diplomacy. However, major sticking points remain unresolved, including sanctions relief, the release of frozen Iranian assets, Tehran's nuclear programme and regional tensions involving Israel and Hezbollah. Meanwhile, fighting continued in Lebanon, where Israeli forces and Hezbollah exchanged fresh attacks across the border. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates.

Provide proof, stay in US: Indians with H1-B visa may not need to panicA new US immigration clarification has brought relief to thousands of Indians on H-1B visas after fears grew that green card applicants may be forced to leave America. USCIS now says many workers providing economic benefit or serving national interest could still remain in the US during the process.

Tortured, electrocuted, poisoned: UP woman murders husband with lover's helpPolice in Moradabad have arrested a woman, her lover, her sister and another man for allegedly killing her husband. The accused tried to pass off the death as suicide after hours of torture.

sports.headline: La Monf's last dance: Gael Monfils bids emotional first-round adieu to Roland Garros Gael Monfils played his final Roland Garros match in 2026, forcing a five-set thriller against Hugo Gaston. Ruud survives five-set epic at French Open, Shelton also advances as Wawrinka bows out French Open: Casper Ruud survived Roman Safiullin in five sets at Roland Garros after blowing five match points and battling dizziness, cramps and heat stress. Animesh Kujur wins 200m gold but misses CWG cut by 0.03s Animesh Kujur won the men's 200m final at the Federation Cup in Ranchi but missed the Commonwealth Games qualifying mark by three hundredths of a second. Federation Cup javelin final raises concern Shivam Lohakare won the Federation Cup men's javelin title in Ranchi with 81.71m, but no Indian met the Commonwealth Games qualifying mark. Will Phil Salt play in RCB vs GT, Qualifier 1? Rajat Patidar shares update IPL 2026: Rajat Patidar said Phil Salt is fit and back in drills ahead of RCB's Qualifier 1 against Gujarat Titans. His return could restore Bengaluru's preferred batting order for a direct shot at the final.

Why Rohit Sharma missed the Padma Shri ceremony in DelhiRohit Sharma's absence from the Padma Shri ceremony in New Delhi surprised many fans, but the former India captain has not missed out on the honour itself. Day 2 Highlights: Ruud, Swiatek, Shelton advance to next round as Wawrinka waves goodbyeFrench Open Day 2 Highlights: Competing in his final French Open, an emotional Stan Wawrinka waved goodbye to French open after first round loss to Jesper de Jong in four-sets (3-6, 6-3, 3-6, 4-6).
